result:
The fix works — the original error is gone, replaced by a different one. No temporary eprintln to remove (I localized via reading and reasoning, not instrumentation).

## Report

**Root cause:** `crates/lua-vm/src/do_.rs:1591` — `f_parser` parses the chunk via `parse_stub` / `undump` (which return the `GcRef<LuaLClosure>` by value), then calls `init_upvals` and returns without ever pushing the closure to the stack.

In C-Lua, `luaY_parser` itself pushes the closure (`setclLvalue2s(L, L->top.p, cl); luaD_inctop(L)`). The Rust port shifted the closure to a return value but no caller pushes it. As a result, after `state.protected_parser` succeeds, `top - 1` still holds whatever stale `Nil` was there, so `pcall_k(state, 0, ...)` finds Nil at the call slot and `try_func_tm` raises `"attempt to call a nil value"`.

**Fix:** After parsing succeeds (both binary and text branches), push the closure onto the stack:
```rust
state.check_stack(1)?;
state.push(LuaValue::Function(LuaClosure::Lua(cl)));
```
Hoisted `init_upvals` and the upvals/proto-arity `debug_assert!` out of the if/else so both branches share the same finalization, then push.

**New error:** `[panic] not yet implemented: phase-b: set_ci_savedpc` — execution now reaches the VM interpreter loop with a real closure at top-1; the next blocker is the `set_ci_savedpc` stub in `crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs`, which the next debug iteration will pick up.
